source.sourcePath: raw/sources/DHF (인허가)/06_(Retrofit) HnX-R1/05_설계개발 검증 및 유효성 확인/08_Cybersecurity/7. A-CLA-HNXR1_Cybersecurity Labeling.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Cybersecurity labeling for the HnX-R1 Digital X-ray Imaging System describing warnings, security configuration, communication ports, logging, SBOM, EOL/EOS, update process, and backup/recovery.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports end-user management of cybersecurity risks associated with HnX-R1 modules and software.
keyPoints: The cybersecurity labeling is limited to the Digital X-ray Imaging System (HnX-R1).
HnX-R1 is indicated for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children and is not to be used for mammography.
HnX-R1 consists of detector, power supply box, battery pack, battery charger, accessories, and HnVUE software, and does not include the X-ray generator.

Cybersecurity controls include endpoint protection, Windows Defender anti-malware, network connection whitelist ACL firewall rules, authentication by ID and password, and restricted unauthorized access.
If a cybersecurity threat is detected, users are instructed to disconnect Bluetooth, turn off the device, and contact the H&abyz service team.
Software and firmware updates cannot be performed in the field and must be performed after collection by the manufacturer or a service center.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity labeling scope
concepts.evidence: This cybersecurity labeling is limited to the Digital X-ray Imaging System (HnX-R1).

concepts.name: Intended use
concepts.evidence: HnX-R1 (Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity risk management
concepts.evidence: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-R1 have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

concepts.name: Security configuration
concepts.evidence: End point protection; Wi-FI communication; Anti-malware: window defender; Firewall / Firewall rules: Network connection white list(ACL)

concepts.name: Allow list and deny list
concepts.evidence: Access to the software can be granted by logging in using an ID and password.

concepts.name: Communication ports
concepts.evidence: In this chapter, all ports of communication are mentioned.

concepts.name: Logging
concepts.evidence: The logs are updated in real time.

concepts.name: SBOM
concepts.evidence: SBOM No Software name Component name Supplier Software level of support by manufacturer version Time stamp (Release date) EOS date Author Description Function

concepts.name: EOL and EOS
concepts.evidence: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS due to the nature of the product.

concepts.name: Software and firmware update process
concepts.evidence: Software (firmware) updates cannot be performed in the field and must be performed after the product has been collected from the manufacturer or a service center.

concepts.name: Backup, retention and recovery
concepts.evidence: When the device runs, it is automatically backed up.

concepts.name: Cybersecurity incident response
concepts.evidence: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

evidence.claim: The document purpose is to provide warnings, precautions, and notices about software cyber risks.
evidence.evidenceText: This is a document that has written warnings, precautions, and notices about software cyber risks.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-R1 (Digital X-ray Imaging System) is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: HnX-R1 is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems and is not for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems. Not to be used for mammography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-R1 system does not include the X-ray generator.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-R1 wired/wireless digital X-ray imaging system consists of Detector, Power supply box, Battery pack, Battery charger, Other Accessories and software for image acquisition and image processing and does not include the X-ray generator.

evidence.claim: The console software is HnVUE.
evidence.evidenceText: The name of the image acquisition and image processing software (Console software) is HnVUE.

evidence.claim: Captured images are saved in DICOM file format and sent to a PACS server.
evidence.evidenceText: saved in DICOM file, a standard for medical imaging. The captured images are sent to the Picture Archiving & Communication System (PACS) server

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity risks were addressed as part of device risk analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-R1 have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

evidence.claim: The intended patient population is adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Intended PATIENT population Adult and children except infants and pregnant women

evidence.claim: The intended users include radiologists and medical imaging specialists.
evidence.evidenceText: Experience: Radiologist, medical imaging specialists

evidence.claim: H&abyz continuously monitors security vulnerabilities applicable to its products.
evidence.evidenceText: H&abyz continuously monitors security vulnerabilities applicable to its products.

evidence.claim: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, users should disconnect Bluetooth, turn off the device, and contact H&abyz service.
evidence.evidenceText: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity configuration includes Windows Defender anti-malware.
evidence.evidenceText: Anti-malware : window defender

evidence.claim: Cybersecurity configuration includes firewall rules using a network connection whitelist ACL.
evidence.evidenceText: Firewall / Firewall rules : Network connection white list(ACL)

evidence.claim: Software access can be granted by logging in with an ID and password.
evidence.evidenceText: Access to the software can be granted by logging in using an ID and password.

evidence.claim: Unauthorized users cannot log in and have limited software access.
evidence.evidenceText: Unauthorized users can't login and have limited access to the software.

evidence.claim: Software installations may only be installed by an authorized person.
evidence.evidenceText: Software installations may only be installed by an authorized person.

evidence.claim: The enabled communication interfaces include Tether Connector, Antenna, and AP Button.
evidence.evidenceText: Tether Connector Enable Both TCP/IP Socket communication; Antenna Enable Wifi Antenna Antenna for Wifi Communication; AP Button Enable NA Button for built-in wireless AP(Access Point) function

evidence.claim: USB-C is disabled in the communication ports table.
evidence.evidenceText: USB-C Disable NA

evidence.claim: Logs are checked through HnVue by selecting View log.
evidence.evidenceText: How to check logs in real-time communication : Run HnVue Click menu in the main windows” View log”

evidence.claim: Logs are updated in real time and saved in .txt format hidden from general users.
evidence.evidenceText: The logs are updated in real time. The log file is saved in a .txt format and is hidden to prevent access by general users

evidence.claim: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS.
evidence.evidenceText: HnVue software does not have a specific EOL/EOS due to the nature of the product.

evidence.claim: The software end date is determined by software change or the 10-year software life cycle.
evidence.evidenceText: The end date is determined by the software change or the software life cycle (10 years).

evidence.claim: Software and firmware updates cannot be performed in the field.
evidence.evidenceText: Software (firmware) updates cannot be performed in the field and must be performed after the product has been collected from the manufacturer or a service center.

evidence.claim: The device automatically backs up setup files and log files when it runs.
evidence.evidenceText: When the device runs, it is automatically backed up. Set up Files, Log Files

evidence.claim: Unauthorized individuals cannot perform backups.
evidence.evidenceText: Unauthorized individuals cannot perform backups

evidence.claim: If setup files are missing or unreadable, the system automatically restores the latest backed-up files.
evidence.evidenceText: If Setup Files does not exist or cannot be read, it automatically restores the latest backed-up files.

reviewFlags.reason: No regulatory authority or jurisdiction is identified in the source text; authoritySignal is only 'general'.
No formal external cybersecurity standard is explicitly cited; DICOM is cited only as a medical imaging file standard.
Several SBOM release dates use abbreviated or inconsistent formats such as '11. 5. 5.', '24. 5. 27.', and '25. 07. 07.', making exact date normalization uncertain.
Source text contains OCR or transcription artifacts such as 'unkonown', 'Abyzr's', broken component names, and punctuation fragments.
